fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 60 is inverted: the clamping block executes when `clamp` is False instead of when it is True, and conversely the no-clamp (extrapolation) path is taken when `clamp=True`.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch body (clamping logic) should be entered, not skipped — the guard `not clamp` is inverted relative to intended semantics.

spec.expr: y__2 >= min(y0, y1) and y__2 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: After the clamping branch executes, y must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__3 >= min(y0, y1) and y__3 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, y__3 (the value after the clamping block) must be within the segment's y range.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1) and __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the returned value must not exceed the endpoint y values.
